title: Savory Thai Eggplant Stir-fry
description: A salty and silky Thai eggplant dish that is perfect for lazy Sunday meals, featuring bicarbonate of soda as a secret ingredient to add extra texture and moisture.

ingredients.name: vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: high smoke point to avoid burning the dish

ingredients.name: fish sauce
ingredients.notes: adds a savory depth to the dish, high-protein-friendly option available

ingredients.name: lime juice
ingredients.notes: balances out the saltiness and acidity

ingredients.name: sugar
ingredients.notes: to balance flavors and enhance sweetness in dishes

ingredients.name: bicarbonate of soda
ingredients.notes: helps with browning and enhances the texture of eggplant

instructions: Preheat a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add oil and swirl to coat the bottom.

Slice the eggplant into rounds about 1/4 inch thick. Season with a pinch of bicarbonate of soda and a bit of salt for extra flavor. Toss gently in a bowl before adding them to the hot wok, ensuring they cook evenly on all sides.
Sauté the onion until translucent, then add minced garlic and cook briefly, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add soy sauce, fish sauce, lime juice, and sugar to the pan. Mix well with the eggplant slices, ensuring they are coated in the sauce mixture. Stir gently to distribute evenly.
Cover the wok partially and allow it to steam for about 10 minutes until most of the liquid evaporates, helping the eggplants become crispier on the outside and softer within.
Reduce heat slightly and continue to stir-fry until the eggplant is tender yet has a slight chewiness. Adjust seasoning with additional salt or sugar if necessary.
Serve hot as a side dish or over rice for an extra protein boost.
